channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Low branches In Car Park, 19th May
item.description:
This tree is overgrown. The branches have grown to the point that is interfering with the car park space. It is difficult to park our vehicles in this spot and would potentially become worser and grow in to the parking space next to it.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Muston Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned van, 19th May
item.description:
This van has been parked in my street for over 5 weeks. It has a flat tyre. It is blocking my view when I reverse off my drive. It is a busy road with lots of pedestrians going to and from Balgowan primary school.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): St James's Avenue, Elmers End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Ongoing illegal parking on Theydon Road, 19th May
item.description:
Cars parked on pavement (blocking for pushchairs/wheelchairs) and double yellow lines/ junctions. These photos were taken at 5.15pm on 19/05/26, there were more (counted 7 cars, this app limits to 3 photos). This happens throughout the week. Please enforce

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Theydon Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Cracked branch about to fall, 19th May
item.description:
Large cracked branch very dangerous

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Hurst Road, Albany Park, Sidcup, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large Bright Sign Placed without Consultation, 19th May
item.description:
Today a large sign was put up across the road from my house. There was no consultation with residents in the area. The sign has large bright lettering and describes the Second World War pillbox. The nature of the sign focuses on the construction and use of the pillbox and features a picture of a bren gun and a soldier. The text seems to glorify war rather than reflect the impact of it on Enfield's civilians. Was planning permission granted for this sign?

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Lavender Hill, Enfield
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Mattress fly tipped on kashgar road, 19th May
item.description:
Endless fly tipping on this road! Weekly dumps of mattresses and other household belongings continue to be left.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Plumstead High Street, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Parked for 3 months, hasn't been moved, 19th May
item.description:
Have checked and MOT on vehicle expired March 26. Hasn't been moved but is parked on street and seems abandoned. No-one I've spoken to locally claims ownership.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Commonwealth Way, Abbey Wood
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Zebra crossing light out, 19th May
item.description:
“Green man” crossing lamp does not illuminate on the westerly side of the northernmost crossing over A206 at James Watt Way (the lamp post aside the northbound traffic, closest to the train track)
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Queens Road, North End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Southwood rd, 19th May
item.description:
More dumped rubbish in Southwood rd under the tree/ bushes

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Woolf Close, Thamesmead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Southwood rd, 19th May
item.description:
Don’t we have any one sweeping the footpaths anymore??? That rubbish has been there for over two weeks ?? Southwood rd footpaths need a good clean and it’s no good sending that little road sweeper that just cleans the middle of the road because all the cars are parked up .

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Woolf Close, Thamesmead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish thrown out on street., 19th May
item.description:
There is a baby cot thrown on the side of the road.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Verdant Lane, Downham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Tree branch needs clearing, 19th May
item.description:
A branch has fallen onto the pavement. We have moved it as an obstruction pending your clearance.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Gordon Road, Elmers End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overgrown vegetation, 19th May
item.description:
Trees are overgrown and encroaching onto the road. Also obscuring a zig zag bend and reduce speed now road sign. The bend is notorious for accidents.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Chislehurst Road, Petts Wood, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped bed frame, mattress, suitcase and bags, 19th May
item.description:
Flytipped furniture etc on pavement. Please can it be removed
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Mcleod Road, Abbey Wood
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Low Branches, 19th May
item.description:
Low branches all along road need trimming back

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Charlton Park Lane, Charlton
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped bed bases, 19th May
item.description:
2 single bed bases have been dumped on pavement. Please can they be removed
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Federation Road, Abbey Wood
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Time plate sprayed, 19th May
item.description:
Time plate sprayed at Lamp Post 08- Permit holders
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Roseberry Street, Bermondsey, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Still rubbish, 19th May
item.description:
Nothing has been cleaned up

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Rutland Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: This sycamore tree needs trimming down as every spring it gets fuller, 19th May
item.description:
It’s right outside our house ( public footpath too) and the amount of green sycamore buds that fall over the pathway when it rains are dangerous for slipping. Our whole garden gets covered inc car on drive. Plus we cannot grow any plants as the tree takes all the light so no sunshine in the area. I’m disabled & my husband is 76. We cannot keep sweeping up

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Mead Way, Hayes, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 4 bags of garden waste in grey refuse sacks next to post box, 19th May
item.description:
Abandoned between 12.45pm-1.45pm today
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Vernham Road, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet
